sections.title: What are Teams?
sections.content: Teams are groups of AI agents working together on related tasks with shared resources, memory, and objectives. They enable collaborative workflows and help organize your automation at scale.
highlights: Shared memory and context across team members

cases.title: Content Marketing Team
cases.description: Coordinated content creation, publishing, and promotion
agents: Content Writer: Creates blog posts and articles
Social Media Manager: Schedules and promotes content
SEO Specialist: Optimizes content for search engines
cases.workflow: Writer creates content → SEO optimizes → Social promotes → Analytics tracks results

cases.title: Customer Support Team
cases.description: Multi-tiered support with escalation and knowledge sharing
cases.workflow: First-line handles → Specialist resolves complex → Manager escalates urgent → Curator updates knowledge

cases.title: Development Team
cases.description: Code review, testing, and deployment automation
cases.workflow: Reviewer checks code → Tester runs tests → Scanner checks security → Manager deploys

features.title: Shared Memory
features.description: Team members can access and update shared context and knowledge
features.details: All agents in a team can read from and write to shared memory, enabling seamless information flow and coordination.
examples: Customer interaction history shared across support agents
Project status updates accessible to all team members
Shared knowledge base that grows with team experience

features.title: Resource Management
features.description: Shared token limits, API quotas, and access permissions
features.details: Teams can share resources efficiently with centralized management of costs and access controls.
examples: Shared daily token budget across team members
Common API key access for external services

features.title: Workflow Coordination
features.description: Orchestrated task execution and handoffs between agents
features.details: Agents can trigger each other's actions and coordinate complex multi-step workflows automatically.

features.title: Team Analytics
features.details: Monitor team performance, resource usage, and collaboration effectiveness with detailed analytics.
examples: Team productivity and task completion rates

steps.title: Create Team
steps.description: Set basic team information and goals

fields.name: Description
fields.example: Creates and promotes engaging content across all marketing channels

fields.name: Token Budget
fields.description: Daily token limit for the team

steps.title: Add Agents
steps.description: Create or assign agents to your team

options.title: Create New Agents
options.description: Build agents specifically for this team

options.title: Assign Existing Agents
options.description: Move agents from other teams or individual assignments

steps.title: Configure Permissions
steps.description: Set access levels and resource sharing

tips: Keep teams focused on related tasks and objectives
Aim for 3-7 agents per team for optimal coordination
Define clear roles and responsibilities for each agent
Set realistic token budgets based on team workload
Monitor resource usage regularly to avoid overruns
Share API keys at team level for cost efficiency
Use team-specific rate limits for external services
Design clear handoff points between agents
Use shared memory for coordination and status updates
Implement error handling and fallback procedures
Document team workflows for easy maintenance
Regularly review team analytics and performance metrics
Optimize agent prompts based on team collaboration patterns
Adjust team composition based on workload changes
Use A/B testing for workflow improvements

solutions: Review and clarify agent role definitions
Improve shared memory structure and usage
Add explicit coordination points in workflows
Use team memory to track task assignments
Analyze token usage patterns by agent
Optimize prompts to reduce token consumption
Adjust team composition and task distribution
Increase budget or implement task prioritization
Check agent health and update configurations
Validate shared memory isn't getting too large
Consider team restructuring or agent retraining
